steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content: For a cone, volume is $V=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2h$. The radius is $4$ cm and the height is $15$ cm, so substitute those values directly.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The volume of a cone is $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$$ Substitute $r = 4$ and $h = 15$: $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (4)^2(15)$$
